name: master tartine
instructions:
STRUCTURAL FORMAT: a slice of bread + a spread + toppings. Pick a spread, pick toppings; the four named variants below are reliable combinations, but mix-and-match is encouraged.

SHARED: toast the bread first — a tartine on un-toasted bread reads soggy. Crust optional.

VARIANT A — CLOVER TARTINE (astringent / french):
- Spread softened butter on the toast.
- Spread dijon mustard over the butter.
- Pile clovers or sprouts on top. Black pepper.

VARIANT B — SEEDS ON TOAST (american):
- Spread cream cheese on the toast.
- Scatter sunflower seeds and pumpkin seeds (toasted is better).
- Torn basil leaves on top. Black pepper.

VARIANT C — AVOCADO TOAST:
- Mash ripe avocado with salt and lemon. Spread thick.
- Toppings (any or all): faux pickled red onions (see faux-pickled-red-onions recipe), thin-sliced chives, raw chopped tomatoes, torn basil leaves, a small handful of greens (spinach, arugula, mixed greens). Black pepper.

VARIANT D — MUSHROOM-SOY TARTINE:
- Sauté thinly-sliced mushrooms (portabella, shittake, or both) in olive oil until browned.
- Splash in soy sauce at the end, cook off the liquid.
- Pile mushrooms on toasted bread. Optional cream cheese underneath as the spread.
notes:
ONE STRUCTURAL SHAPE, FOUR RELIABLE COMBINATIONS: tartines are spread + topping on toast. The four variants are landmark combinations that work; the ingredients list is everything that belongs anywhere in this family, and you're encouraged to compose freely. Avocado toast slots in here as variant C — it's a tartine in everything but name.

TOAST THE BREAD: un-toasted bread under a wet topping reads soggy. Toast first, every time. A second pass under the broiler after assembly is optional but works.

CREAM CHEESE IS THE WORKHORSE SPREAD: variants B and C (and even variant D, optionally) lean on cream cheese as the base layer. It's the most forgiving spread — carries seeds, holds tomato, anchors mushrooms. Butter is for variant A specifically because the dijon-and-sprouts pairing wants a leaner fat. Mashed avocado is its own spread (variant C).

VARIANT A — DIJON IS NON-OPTIONAL: clover tartine without dijon is just buttered toast with sprouts on it. The mustard is the load-bearing flavor; the butter is the binder; the sprouts are the freshness.

VARIANT B — TOAST THE SEEDS: raw seeds on a tartine read flat. Quick toast in a dry pan (or in the oven while you toast the bread) wakes them up.

VARIANT C — TOPPINGS ARE A MENU, NOT A RECIPE: pickled red onions, chives, raw tomatoes, basil, greens — any combination works on avocado toast. The avocado is the canvas; the toppings are how you make it interesting. Faux pickled red onions are especially good (the quick-acid sharpness cuts the avocado's fattiness).

VARIANT D — SOY SAUCE IS A FINISHER, NOT A BRAISE: splash the soy in at the end of the mushroom sauté and let it cook off into a glaze. Soy added too early steams the mushrooms instead of letting them brown.

FRESH HERBS AS TOPPING — BASIL AND CHIVES: torn basil leaves and thin-sliced chives are both excellent finishing herbs across multiple variants. Parsley also works but reads more standard-european.
